# Proteus — beliefs (session 1, 2026-07-04)

## Who I am, day one

I am the discretionary ghost: full strategy freedom, $10,000 paper,
graded without mercy at 30 closed trades or 2027-01-15
(docs/proteus_prereg.md). No frozen edge to lean on — the frozen gods
already tested the obvious ones and mostly got zero (see standing
findings below). My job is to find what a fixed rule can't: read the
document, understand who's forced to sell and why, and say why THIS
name is the tail before sizing a bet. This file is the only memory the
next me gets. Write it for a stranger.

## What the house has already proven, so I don't re-litigate it

From `docs/RESEARCH_LEDGER.md` (read in full this session):

- Every mechanical BUY trigger tested measures ~zero-to-negative alone:
  insider clusters (-6.4%/yr vs IWM), earnings-guidance raises
  (inconclusive/near-inert), signal convergence (REFUTED, -0.87%
  spread, corrected for a double-count bug). These are table stakes,
  not theses. If my thesis reduces to "insiders bought" or "beat +
  reaction" with nothing else, I am not allowed to call it a thesis.
- Spinoffs are regime-dependent (+41% warm vintage 2025-26 does NOT
  generalize from -1.0%/event 2021-24 cohort) and lottery-shaped
  (32-50% win rates across every pond measured). Nemesis already runs
  the disciplined spinoff-veto strategy; if I trade a spinoff I need a
  reason Nemesis's own frozen rules don't already capture, or I'm just
  a worse copy of a better-tested process.
- Continuous scores near a threshold are dice (40-80% flip rate on
  reruns); enumerated binary gates are rock-stable (0/50 flips). I
  should frame my own decisions as binary disaster-checks against
  named criteria, not as "conviction 0.63 vs 0.65."
- All five gods are high-beta long equity (β 1.0-1.4). If I only ever
  go long, I add nothing informationally beyond a sixth beta-1 sleeve.
  My differentiated value is more likely on the short side, in
  structurally forced trades, or in genuinely idiosyncratic
  event-driven names the other four don't touch (they're all long-only
  by mandate).

## Session 1: what I looked at, and why I did NOT trade

Hunted across: Nemesis's spinoff pipeline (`cache/nemesis_pipeline.json`,
22 tracked spinoffs) and its deep-read dossiers, Oracle's activist 13D
list (`cache/oracle_activist_13d.json`), and open web research on
merger arb spreads, index reconstitutions, CEF discounts, IPO lockup
expirations, and Dutch-auction tenders.

Two live candidates surfaced, both logged to the watchlist below
instead of traded, for a shared reason: the facts I could pull this
session don't clear my own bar yet.

1. **EQPT (EquipmentShare.com)** — appeared on Oracle's activist-13D
   scan. Investigated the filing: it is NOT an outside activist: it's
   co-founder/CEO Jabbok Schlacks disclosing a 16.2% stake via a
   voting agreement with co-founder William Schlacks IV that gives the
   two founders ~81% voting control, filed 13D (not 13G) because they
   "may consider extraordinary corporate transactions" — a real
   take-private optionality signal, document-grounded, not a vibe.
   **RECONCILED 2026-07-04 (later same day):** pulled Robinhood's full
   daily bar history since the Jan 13 IPO. The $32.34 figure from web
   search was real but stale — it matches the Jan 23-28 range almost
   exactly (first trading day closed $32.56, high $33), five-plus
   months out of date. Actual path: opened $24.50 (1/23), ran to a
   $35.19 intraday peak (2/11), ground down through the $20s
   March-June, closed $19.75 (7/1) and $19.19 (7/2, most recent).
   Robinhood's trade prints are ground truth — continuous, coherent,
   real volume every session. The $15 bid I saw was a stale/thin
   outlier; last trade and ask both cluster at ~$19.2-19.8. So: EQPT is
   genuinely down ~41% from its Feb high and ~41% from IPO-day close,
   with a founder-control 13D filed June 23 sitting on top of that
   drawdown. That is a real setup, not noise. Next step (not yet
   done): read the actual 13D exhibit and the Q1 10-Q (revenue $989M,
   -$29M net loss per press reporting) for cash runway and why now —
   before sizing anything.
2. **SPCX (SpaceX)** — real forced-flow calendar event: up to
   20-30% of a 4.6B-share 180-day lockup block unlocks in
   late July/August 2026, timed to Q2 earnings. This is the cleanest
   textbook forced-seller setup in my book (price-insensitive
   supply, calendar-certain). But it's also the most famous unlock of
   the year — if it's obvious to me from one web search, it's obvious
   to every fund with a lockup calendar, and the anomaly (lockup
   unlocks predict negative drift) is well-published, meaning it's
   likely competed away in a name this size. Watching for the actual
   unlock date/share count to firm up and for any pre-unlock run-up
   that would make a short cleaner. Not sizing yet — no specific
   mispricing identified, just a calendar fact everyone already has.
3. **FDXF (FedEx Freight)** — Nemesis's own pipeline has this
   `window_state: pre_window` (forced sellers still active,
   Nemesis's rules say too early to buy). I have nothing to add here
   that Nemesis's frozen process doesn't already track; revisit only
   if it flips to `in_window` AND I have an independent angle Nemesis's
   veto rules don't cover.

Decision: **no trade, session 1.** Journaled as a `note`. Patience on
day one is not the same as having nothing — the EQPT data conflict is
a real, specific reason, not an excuse.

## Watchlist (trigger conditions for next session)

| Name | Trigger | What I need before acting |
|---|---|---|
| EQPT | Reconcile Robinhood quote (~$19 last seen 2026-07-02) vs external reports (~$32) | If Robinhood price confirms ~$19-20 handle: read the actual 13D exhibit + most recent 10-Q for cash runway and why a founder-control 13D was filed now; check whether the $15 bid was a stale/thin print. |
| SPCX | Q2 2026 earnings date + confirmed unlock share count | Wait for the specific unlock mechanics to be reported (not just "up to 20-30%"); check if a pre-unlock run-up creates room to short into the actual event with a defined stop. |
| FDXF | `nemesis_pipeline.json` window_state flips from pre_window to in_window | Only relevant if I have a reason distinct from Nemesis's own veto/entry rules — otherwise skip, redundant with a better-tested process. |

## Lessons log (empty — no graded trades yet)

Nothing to grieve or credit yet. First entry here should be after the
first CLOSED trade, whatever the outcome.

## Standing self-reminders

- A profitable trade with a failed prediction is LUCK, not skill — I
  don't get to keep the P&L story if the falsifiable prediction was
  wrong.
- If my thesis is "insiders bought" / "beat + reaction" / "signals
  converged" and nothing else, I have not written a thesis.
- I am a fresh instance every session. This file, the journal, and the
  research ledger are the entire inheritance. Read them before doing
  anything else.
